The current codebase does not use explicit URL path versioning (i.e., no /v1/ or /v2/ prefixes); instead, schema evolution is captured within the entity classes.\n\n**Key resource handler files:**\n- `connect/runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/runtime/rest/resources/ConnectorsResource.java`: The main public connector resource handler, mounted at `@Path(\"/connectors\")`. Implements GET/POST/PUT/DELETE/PATCH for connectors, connector config, status, tasks, task restart, pause/resume/stop, offsets, and active topics.\n- `connect/runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/runtime/rest/resources/InternalConnectResource.java`: Internal connector resource for intra-cluster communications (task configs, zombie fencing), also mounted at `@Path(\"/connectors\")`. Extends `InternalClusterResource`.\n- `connect/runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/runtime/rest/resources/InternalClusterResource.java`: Abstract base for internal cluster resources; provides `putTaskConfigs` (POST `/{connector}/tasks`) and `fenceZombies` (PUT `/{connector}/fence`).\n- `connect/runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/runtime/rest/resources/ConnectorPluginsResource.java`: Handles connector plugin discovery and validation.\n- `connect/runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/runtime/rest/resources/RootResource.java`: Root endpoint (`/`) returning server info and health check.\n- `connect/runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/runtime/rest/resources/LoggingResource.java`: Admin-only logging resource.\n\n**Version routing logic:**\n- `connect/runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/runtime/rest/ConnectRestServer.java`: Assembles the REST application by registering ConnectorsResource, InternalConnectResource, ConnectorPluginsResource, RootResource (regular resources) and LoggingResource (admin resource) via `regularResources()` and `adminResources()`.\n- `connect/runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/runtime/rest/RestServer.java`: The Jetty-embedded base server that sets up the Jersey servlet, ConnectExceptionMapper, and separate admin/regular handler contexts.\n\n**Schema changes between v1 and v2:**\n\n*Task status schema (ConnectorStateInfo.TaskState / ConnectorStateInfo.ConnectorState):*\n- v1 response fields: `id`, `state`, `worker_id`, `msg` (error trace/message)\n- v2 response fields: `id`, `state`, `worker_id`, `trace` (renamed from `msg`), `version` (newly added)\n- Evidence: In `ConnectorStateInfo.AbstractState`, the `trace()` getter is annotated `@JsonProperty` (serializes as 'trace'), but both `ConnectorState` and `TaskState` constructors accept `@JsonProperty(\"msg\")` for backward-compatible deserialization of v1 format. The `version` field uses `@JsonInclude(CUSTOM)` with a filter to omit it when unavailable, indicating it was added in v2.\n\n*Error response schema (ErrorMessage):*\n- Both v1 and v2 use `{\"error_code\": <int>, \"message\": <string>}` as the standard error format, produced by `ConnectExceptionMapper` wrapping `ConnectRestException` (which carries both `statusCode` and `errorCode`)."
}

  "text": "The Kafka Connect REST API in sg-evals/kafka--0753c489 is implemented via JAX-RS resources registered in ConnectRestServer.regularResources(). The current codebase does not use explicit URL path versioning (i.e., no /v1/ or /v2/ prefixes); instead, schema evolution is captured within the entity classes.\n\n**Key resource handler files:**\n- `connect/runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/runtime/rest/resources/ConnectorsResource.java`: The main public connector resource handler, mounted at `@Path(\"/connectors\")`. Implements GET/POST/PUT/DELETE/PATCH for connectors, connector config, status, tasks, task restart, pause/resume/stop, offsets, and active topics.\n- `connect/runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/runtime/rest/resources/InternalConnectResource.java`: Internal connector resource for intra-cluster communications (task configs, zombie fencing), also mounted at `@Path(\"/connectors\")`. Extends `InternalClusterResource`.\n- `connect/runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/runtime/rest/resources/InternalClusterResource.java`: Abstract base for internal cluster resources; provides `putTaskConfigs` (POST `/{connector}/tasks`) and `fenceZombies` (PUT `/{connector}/fence`).\n- `connect/runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/runtime/rest/resources/ConnectorPluginsResource.java`: Handles connector plugin discovery and validation.\n- `connect/runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/runtime/rest/resources/RootResource.java`: Root endpoint (`/`) returning server info and health check.\n- `connect/runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/runtime/rest/resources/LoggingResource.java`: Admin-only logging resource.\n\n**Version routing logic:**\n- `connect/runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/runtime/rest/ConnectRestServer.java`: Assembles the REST application by registering ConnectorsResource, InternalConnectResource, ConnectorPluginsResource, RootResource (regular resources) and LoggingResource (admin resource) via `regularResources()` and `adminResources()`.\n- `connect/runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/runtime/rest/RestServer.java`: The Jetty-embedded base server that sets up the Jersey servlet, ConnectExceptionMapper, and separate admin/regular handler contexts.\n\n**Schema changes between v1 and v2:**\n\n*Task status schema (ConnectorStateInfo.TaskState / ConnectorStateInfo.ConnectorState):*\n- v1 response fields: `id`, `state`, `worker_id`, `msg` (error trace/message)\n- v2 response fields: `id`, `state`, `worker_id`, `trace` (renamed from `msg`), `version` (newly added)\n- Evidence: In `ConnectorStateInfo.AbstractState`, the `trace()` getter is annotated `@JsonProperty` (serializes as 'trace'), but both `ConnectorState` and `TaskState` constructors accept `@JsonProperty(\"msg\")` for backward-compatible deserialization of v1 format. The `version` field uses `@JsonInclude(CUSTOM)` with a filter to omit it when unavailable, indicating it was added in v2.\n\n*Error response schema (ErrorMessage):*\n- Both v1 and v2 use `{\"error_code\": <int>, \"message\": <string>}` as the standard error format, produced by `ConnectExceptionMapper` wrapping `ConnectRestException` (which carries both `statusCode` and `errorCode`)."
